Container.Name: Server-Manager-Portal
Container.Overview:
A self-hosted Plex server management portal with user onboarding, access expiry, analytics, status monitoring, and admin tools.
After install, open the WebUI and sign in with your Plex admin account, then configure Plex token and SMTP in Settings.

Config.Name: Config Directory
Config.Description: Persistent JSON data (users, settings, caches, logs). Container path: /app/config

Config.Name: Backup Directory
Config.Description: Rolling backup snapshots created from Settings. Container path: /app/backup

Config.Name: JWT Secret
Config.Description: Required session signing secret (minimum 32 characters). Generate with: node -e "console.log(require('crypto').randomBytes(48).toString('hex'))"

Config.Name: Public Base URL
Config.Description: Public HTTPS URL for email links (no trailing slash). For subpath hosting include the path, e.g. https://plex.example.com/portal

Config.Name: Base Path
Config.Description: Optional URL prefix when hosted under a subpath (e.g. /portal). Leave empty for root hosting.

Config.Name: Force Secure Cookies
Config.Description: Set to true when the portal is served over HTTPS (reverse proxy).

Config.Name: Allow Private Integration URLs
Config.Description: Allow Sonarr/Radarr/Tautulli URLs on LAN/private IPs (recommended for homelab).

Config.Name: Setup Token
Config.Description: Optional one-time token for remote first-time setup.

Config.Name: Plex OAuth Client ID
Config.Description: Optional fixed Plex OAuth client id (auto-generated on first run if empty).

Config.Name: Config Data Directory
Config.Description: Internal runtime data path (normally leave as /app/config).

Config.Name: Internal Port
Config.Description: Application listen port inside the container.
